title: Ensure that the --audit-log-maxsize argument is set to 100 or as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --audit-log-maxsize argument is set to 100 or as appropriate
input_row.description: Rotate log files on reaching 100 MB or as appropriate.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes automatically rotates the log files. Retaining old log files ensures that you would have sufficient log data available for carrying out any investigation or correlation. If you have set file size of 100 MB and the number of old log files to keep as 10, you would approximate have 1 GB of log data that you could potentially use for your analysis.
input_row.audit: Run the following command: ps -ef | grep federation-apiserver Verify that the --audit-log-maxsize argument is set to 100 or as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Edit the deployment specs and set --audit-log-maxsize=100 to 100 or as appropriate. kubectl edit deployments federation-apiserver-deployment --namespace=federation-system Impact: None
input_row.default_value: By default, auditing is not enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_audit_log_maxsize_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the configuration of the API server.
- Specifically, verify the `--audit-log-maxsize` flag in the API server deployment.
- The command to retrieve the API server deployment configuration is:
  ```bash
  kubectl get deployment kube-apiserver -n kube-system -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].args}'
  ```
- The expected output should include `--audit-log-maxsize=100` or an appropriate value as per your organizational policy.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The output contains `--audit-log-maxsize=100`.
  - **Fail:** The output does not contain `--audit-log-maxsize=100` or is set to a value greater than 100.
